TOR.name: Toronto
_needs_data: network_equipment: $0 — verify whether these DCs have shared switching costs or whether networking is bundled into the colo/facility contract
dc_ops: $0 — verify whether DC ops labor is tracked separately or rolled into facility cost

notes: DC cost overheads from CPQ finance spreadsheet. support_ops: data-driven from 6-month Jira worklog analysis (Nov 2025–May 2026), clean rate × 2.16× overhead × $80/hr. dc_equipment: NOT POPULATED — hardware amortization missing, see cost_component_methodology. network_ops + compute_ops: NOT POPULATED — specialist labor untracked, see cost_component_methodology. MTL/TOR: network_equipment and dc_ops may be missing — see _needs_data flags. capacity_model section added for step-cost planning. Fusion sb_datacenter IDs included for pricebook joins.

dc_equipment.description: Hardware amortization — the monthly cost of owning the physical device itself.
dc_equipment.current_state: NOT POPULATED — $0 everywhere. This is a real cost gap.
dc_equipment.data_needed: Asset register or CPQ product cost per device type

network_equipment.description: Amortized monthly share of shared switching/routing infrastructure per device.
network_equipment.current_state: PARTIALLY POPULATED — $59 for most USD DCs, $0 for MTL/TOR.
network_equipment.composition: Includes: ToR/distribution switch amortization, patch panel hardware, fiber/copper cabling, SFP optics

port_intensity.firewall: 3-4 ports (WAN, LAN, HA, MGMT) — verify: may be 1.5-2x baseline
port_intensity.switch: 2 uplink ports on parent distribution switch — baseline
port_intensity.SAN: 2-4 FC or iSCSI ports — baseline to 2x
port_intensity.Virtual: 0 — vSwitch is software, cost is in host server

network_equipment.data_needed: Finance: total monthly switching capex amortization + maintenance per DC / device count

dc_ops.description: DC operations labor — physical hands, facilities overhead, NOC desk share allocated to physical devices.
dc_ops.current_state: PARTIALLY POPULATED — $19-28 for USD DCs, $0 for MTL/TOR.
dc_ops.composition: Includes: DC technician time (hands-and-eyes, racking, cabling), facility overhead (security, HVAC management), amortized across all physical devices in that DC
dc_ops.data_needed: Finance/HR: DC technician headcount + salary per DC; Facilities: monthly overhead per DC

network_ops.description: Network engineering labor — specialist time managing network device configs, BGP/OSPF, firewall rules, VLAN changes.
network_ops.current_state: NOT POPULATED — $0 everywhere. Real cost exists but is untracked.
applies_to_ranked: firewall — highest: rule management, policy reviews, change requests
switch — medium: VLAN management, firmware, port configs
loadbalancer — medium: VIP management, health checks, SSL certs
Connectivity — medium: BGP, routing, circuit management
network_ops.data_needed: HR: network engineer headcount + salary; Jira: track time by team specialty (NOC vs Net-Eng vs Compute)

compute_ops.description: Compute/virtualization engineering labor — hypervisor management, VM provisioning, snapshot policies, capacity planning.
compute_ops.current_state: NOT POPULATED — $0 everywhere. Real cost exists but is untracked.
applies_to_ranked: Virtual — highest: vMotion, snapshots, template management, capacity
clustering — medium: cluster health, failover testing, quorum
backup — medium: backup job management, restore testing
server — low: only if managed OS/patching is included
compute_ops.data_needed: HR: compute engineer headcount + salary; Jira: track time by team specialty

support_ops.description: Service desk labor — ticket handling, change requests, customer communication.
support_ops.current_state: POPULATED — derived from 6-month Jira worklog analysis with outlier removal and 2.16x overhead multiplier.

capacity_model._description: Step-cost planning parameters. Use amortized per-device rates for pricing (correct in aggregate). Use capacity triggers to forecast infrastructure purchases when growing the portfolio.

capacity_reserve_pct.note: Add 15% to total cost for infrastructure refresh cycles and step-cost events. Adjust upward (20-25%) if the DC is near capacity; downward (10%) if headroom is ample.

server.trigger: Every ~40 servers fills a 42U rack → new rack + cabling + TOR switch purchase

Virtual.trigger: Every ~20-30 VMs fills a typical host → new hypervisor server purchase

firewall.trigger: Port exhaustion on distribution switches is more likely trigger than rack space

switch.trigger: Same as server for rack; distribution switch ports for uplinks

colocation.trigger: Floor space and power capacity in the DC
colocation.trigger_cost_estimate: Cost is per-cabinet; Aptum's cost is facility lease + power

SAN.rack_units_per_device: 2-8U per array, amortized across many LUNs
SAN.trigger: Array capacity (TB); each shelf expansion is ~$10,000-30,000

pricing_guidance.use_amortized_rates_for: all standard pricing — correct in expectation across portfolio
pricing_guidance.use_step_cost_model_for: large deals (>50 units of same type) — check DC headroom first
pricing_guidance.capacity_check_cadence: quarterly — track rack utilization, port utilization, power headroom per DC
